name: DURHAM LAND REGISTRY 7
units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a 1978 metric log (1:50); depth = base of interval below ground

intervals.verbatim: Firm orangy brown/grey/yellow mottled SANDY CLAY with coal particles and hair roots
Mod compact yellowish brown fine to medium grained SAND with pockets of silty clay.
Firm brown VERY CLAYEY SAND/SILTY CLAY, with local orangy brown mottling.
Mod compact grey/brown slightly silty fine grained SAND with occasional coal washings up to fine gravel size.
Mod compact grey/brown slightly silty medium grained SAND with occasional coal washings.
Firm brown mottled laminated SILTY CLAY.
Compact grey/brown SILTY fine to medium grained SAND with occasional bands of sandy coarse SILT.
Compact grey/brown SILTY fine to medium grained SAND with occasional small pockets of clayey silt.
Compact grey/brown VERY SILTY CLAY/CLAYEY SILT.
Firm becoming stiff grey/brown VERY SILTY laminated CLAY with light grey coarse silt partings.
Compact grey fine to coarse rounded GRAVEL with cobbles and boulders.
Firm to stiff dark grey SILTY and SANDY CLAY with numerous fine to coarse sub-rounded and angular gravel.
annotations: 'Refusal' noted in original at base of the shell & claycutter boring (~20 m)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff to very stiff dark grey SILTY and SANDY CLAY with numerous fine to coarse sub rounded and angular gravel, becoming hard with numerous sandstone fragments below 26.2m
Hard grey SILTY and SANDY CLAY with numerous sandstone fragments up to boulder size.
Very dense yellowish brown fragments of weak micaceous sandstone with traces of friable sandy clay. Highly weathered sandstone.
annotations: weathered rockhead; base of shell & claycutter boring, 'Hole terminated at 29.10m'

intervals.verbatim: 29.1 to 29.5m Yellow/brown fine to medium grained very thin to thin bedded mod.weathered weak to mod. weak SANDSTONE.
annotations: interface level 69.55 m at top (29.1 m)
start of rotary air-flush cored record (pages 4-5); 0-29.1 m was open-hole, 'See Record of shell and claycutter borehole'
Note: All cores micaceous especially along bedding planes
water: No noticeable water seepage whilst drilling

intervals.verbatim: 29.5 to 30.1m Brown becoming grey thinly laminated to laminated mod.weathered very weak SILTSTONE.
annotations: interface level 69.15 m at top (29.5 m)

intervals.verbatim: 30.10 to 39.50m Yellow/brown with grey/brown bands fine to medium grained generally thin to medium bedded moderately weathered weak to moderately strong SANDSTONE. Locally jointed and highly weathered recovered as angular gravel sized sst.fragments in a sand matrix from 32.1 to 32.3m, 33.2 to 33.3m, 36.5 to 36.7m, 37.0 to 37.3m. Thin carbonaceous sand band from 39.3 to 39.4m. Vertically jointed in zones from 35.8 to 36.3m and 36.7 to 37.0m
annotations: thin carbonaceous sand band 39.3-39.4 m is a bedding parting within the sandstone, not a coal seam

notes: 1978 metric site-investigation log for Durham Land Registry BH No.7 (Job FGE/811). TWO records for the same hole: (a) shell & claycutter (S&CC 152mm), 0-29.10 m, dated 29/11-8.12.78, pages 1-3, transcribed as intervals 1-16; (b) rotary air-flush (152/76 mm), dated 17/18.12.78, pages 4-5, transcribed as intervals 17-19 - it records 0-29.1 m as open-hole drilling ('See Record of shell and claycutter borehole') and provides the cored bedrock 29.1-39.5 m. Combined record runs 0-39.50 m (matches index 39.5 m). Sequence: glacial/fluvial drift (topsoil, sandy clays, sands with coal washings, silty clays, gravel with cobbles/boulders, stiff-to-hard till with sandstone fragments) to ~27.8 m, over weathered Coal Measures rockhead - highly weathered micaceous SANDSTONE / SILTSTONE / SANDSTONE to 39.5 m. No named coal seams and no COAL bed present; 'coal particles'/'coal washings' are detrital in the drift, and the 39.3-39.4 m carbonaceous band is a thin sand parting, so seam_marks is empty. Interface (reduced) levels on all interfaces cross-check exactly against GROUND LEVEL 98.65 m (base depth = 98.65 - level); no clerk arithmetic slips found. 'Refusal' marked at the base of the S&CC boring (~20 m). Water: page 1 'Please refer to Section 5 of the written text'; page 4 'No noticeable water seepage whilst drilling'; water-strike boxes otherwise blank. Page 6 is a 1:500 sketch location plan (Appendix B Sheet 2, DRG FGE/811/1) with no strata. SPT N-values and core TCR/SCR/RQD run data appear in the sample columns but are not part of the lithological record; a few are noted in interval annotations. All lithology text read cleanly; no illegible runs.
